text.title: User Interviews Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding User Interviews. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every research project starts from a question and the decision that question will inform; questions without decisions are skipped.
rule.rationale: Research without a decision producer drifts into theatre.

rule.statement: Interview guides are semi-structured — fixed topic blocks, open follow-up — and capped at 45 to 60 minutes.
rule.rationale: Pure structured kills depth; pure unstructured loses comparability.

rule.statement: Synthesis quotes participants verbatim with timestamps; paraphrase only with a clear marker.
rule.rationale: Paraphrased quotes lose signal and inherit researcher bias.

rule.statement: Sample size is justified by saturation, not a fixed number; stop only when two consecutive interviews add no new themes.
rule.rationale: Fixed sample sizes either over- or under-sample reality.

rule.statement: Consent forms cover recording, sharing, and retention; PII is removed from transcripts before sharing beyond the research team.
rule.rationale: Sharing raw PII breaches ethics and may breach law.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-user-interviews.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
